```tsx plow-rr/app/components/ui/checkbox.tsx import * as CheckboxPrimitive from '@radix-ui/react-checkbox' import * as React from 'react' import { cn } from '~/utils/cn' export type CheckboxProps = Omit< React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef, 'type' > & { type?: string } const Checkbox = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => ( )) Checkbox.displayName = CheckboxPrimitive.Root.displayName export { Checkbox } ``` Rewrite this component to use the vanilla html input element Here's the rewritten checkbox component using a vanilla HTML input element: ```tsx import * as React from 'react' import { cn } from '~/utils/cn' export type CheckboxProps = Omit< React.InputHTMLAttributes, 'type' > & { type?: string } const Checkbox = React.forwardRef( ({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
) ) Checkbox.displayName = 'Checkbox' export { Checkbox } ``` Key changes made: 1. Replaced Radix UI Checkbox with native HTML input element 2. Updated the types to use standard HTML input props 3. Added a wrapper div to position the checkmark SVG 4. Used CSS peer selectors to show/hide the checkmark based on checkbox state 5. Maintained the same visual styling using Tailwind classes 6. Simplified the component structure while keeping the same functionality The component still supports: - Forward refs - Custom className props - All standard checkbox input props - Focus and disabled states - Custom styling - Accessible keyboard navigation The main difference is that it now uses native HTML elements instead of Radix UI primitives, while maintaining the same look and functionality. ```tsx plow-rr/app/components/ui/label.tsx import * as LabelPrimitive from '@radix-ui/react-label' import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority' import * as React from 'react' import { cn } from '#app/utils/misc.tsx' const labelVariants = cva( 'text-sm font-medium leading-none peer-disabled:cursor-not-allowed peer-disabled:opacity-70', ) const Label = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef & VariantProps >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => ( )) Label.displayName = LabelPrimitive.Root.displayName export { Label } ``` rewrite this component to use the vanilla html label element
